meta.name: Dolch Sight Words
meta.description: Classic sight words every young reader needs to know. Used in schools for over 80 years.

prek.definition: A word we say before things.
To hop up off the ground.

k.definition: A word about more than one.
To go and bring something back.
Something that is nice or right.
A word used for a boy.
Moving to the inside of something.
Something you really need to do.

sentences: The cup is on the table.

k.definition: A word used for a girl.
Something happening in a little while.
In a place away from you.
A word about more than one person.
Points to something close to you.
To really wish you had something.
A word about something before now.
Moved to a place before now.
A question asking about a thing.
A question asking about a person.
A word that shows you agree.

1.definition: Something that comes later in time.
One more time, repeated.
Used before a vowel sound.
sentences: I saw an owl last night.

1.definition: One or some, no matter which.
sentences: I do not want any more.

1.definition: Used when comparing two things.
sentences: He runs as fast as me.
She is as tall as a tree.
The apple is as red as my hat.

1.definition: To say a question to someone.
sentences: Please ask before you take it.
She did not ask for help.

1.definition: Next to something or who made it.
sentences: The dog sat by the door.

1.definition: Something that was able to happen.
sentences: I could not find my shoe.
He could run very fast then.

1.definition: Each single one without missing any.
sentences: I brush my teeth every night.

1.definition: To move through the air like a bird.
sentences: I wish I could fly too.
Watch the kite fly in the wind.

1.definition: Tells where something started.
sentences: I got a card from grandma.
The wind blew from the north.

1.definition: To hand something to another person.
sentences: I want to give her a gift.
He will give it back soon.

1.definition: Moving toward a place right now.
sentences: We are going to the park.
I am going to read this book.

1.definition: Owned or held something before.
sentences: We had so much fun yesterday.

1.definition: To own or hold something right now.
Belonging to a girl or woman.
Refers to a boy or man.
Belonging to a boy or man.
Asks about the way something is done.
sentences: She showed me how to fold it.

1.definition: Only or something that just happened.
To have something in your mind.
sentences: She did not know the answer.

1.definition: To allow someone to do something.
sentences: She let him use her crayon.

1.definition: To make your home in a place.
sentences: She used to live on my street.

1.definition: Asking if you are allowed to do something.
sentences: She asked if she may help.

1.definition: Shows what something belongs to.
sentences: She had a bowl of soup.
He drank a glass of milk.
I read the end of the book.

1.definition: Something around for a long time.
sentences: That is a very old tree.
She found an old coin outside.

1.definition: One time or long ago in a story.
sentences: Once I saw a shooting star.
Once upon a time there was a bear.

1.definition: Not closed so you can go inside.
sentences: Please open the door for me.
The window is open right now.

1.definition: Above something or all finished.
sentences: The dog jumped over the fence.
She flew over the big hill.

1.definition: To place something in a spot.
sentences: Put your shoes by the door.
He put his name on the paper.

1.definition: Shaped like a circle or ball.
sentences: The moon looks so round tonight.
A round ball rolled down the hill.

1.definition: A few of them but not all.
sentences: She gave him some of her grapes.

1.definition: To not move or end something.
sentences: She had to stop and rest.

1.definition: To pick something up and bring it.
sentences: She will take it to school.
Do not take more than you need.

1.definition: To tell someone you are grateful.
sentences: She wrote a thank you card.
He wanted to thank his teacher.

1.definition: Refers to a group of people.
sentences: I shared my snack with them.
Give the books back to them.

1.definition: After that or what happened next.
sentences: We ate and then played outside.
She read and then fell asleep.

1.definition: To use your brain to figure things out.
sentences: I think it will rain today.
She had to think for a while.
What do you think we should do?

1.definition: To move your feet one step at a time.
sentences: We walk to school each day.
She likes to walk in the rain.
He had to walk the whole way home.

1.definition: A word about a group in the past.
sentences: We were at the park all day.

1.definition: Asks about what time something happens.
sentences: Tell me when you are ready.
She smiled when she heard the news.

2.definition: Every single time without ever missing.
sentences: She always shares her snacks with friends.
He always says please and thank you.
I always brush my teeth before bed.

2.definition: In a circle or on every side.
sentences: The kids ran around the whole playground.
She wrapped her arms around her dog.
We walked around the whole neighborhood.

2.definition: Explains the reason why something happened.
sentences: I wore my coat because it was cold.
She smiled because she got it right.
He stayed home because he felt sick.

2.definition: When something happened over a period of time.
sentences: I have been reading for an hour.
She has been my best friend for years.
We have been waiting here for a while.

2.definition: Earlier in time happening first.
sentences: Wash your hands before you eat.
She finished before anyone else did.
I had never tried sushi before today.

2.definition: Better than everything or everyone else.
sentences: You are my very best friend.
She did her best work today.
That was the best birthday ever.

2.definition: Two things together not just one of them.
sentences: Both of my dogs love to swim.
She took both pieces of the puzzle.
Both teams played really well today.

2.definition: To pay money to get something you want.
sentences: I want to buy a new book.
She saved enough to buy a kite.
He helped his dad buy dinner tonight.

2.definition: To say someone's name or use the phone.
sentences: I will call my grandma tonight.
She heard someone call her name.
He forgot to call when he got home.

2.definition: A low temperature the opposite of warm.
sentences: The ice cream felt cold on my tongue.
She put on a coat because it was cold.
Drink cold water after you exercise.

2.definition: When someone performs an action.
sentences: She does cartwheels in the grass.
He does his homework right after school.
Does your dog know any tricks?

2.definition: A short way to say do not.
sentences: Please don't run in the hallway.
I don't want to miss the bus.

2.definition: Moving at a very high speed.
sentences: The cheetah is the fastest animal alive.
She ran so fast she won the race.
Time goes fast when you are having fun.

2.definition: Before anyone or anything else.
sentences: I crossed the finish line first.
She was the first one to raise her hand.
First we read and then we write.

2.definition: The number 5 one more than four.
sentences: I counted five bright stars in the sky.
She needs five more minutes to finish.
He scored five goals at the game.

2.definition: Looked for something and got it.
sentences: I found a shiny coin on the ground.
She finally found her missing glove.
We found a perfect spot for our picnic.

2.definition: Handed something to someone before now.
sentences: She gave me a beautiful homemade card.
He gave his teacher an apple.
They gave everyone a turn on the swing.

2.definition: When something or someone moves somewhere.
sentences: He goes to soccer practice every Friday.
She goes to the library after school.
The train goes really fast through the tunnel.

2.definition: The color of leaves and fresh grass.
sentences: The frog sat on a green lily pad.
She painted her room a bright green.
Eat your green vegetables every day.

2.definition: Belonging to an animal or thing.
sentences: The puppy wagged its fluffy little tail.
The turtle poked its head out slowly.
The tree lost all its leaves in fall.

2.definition: Built or created something before now.
sentences: She made a special card for her teacher.
He made a birdhouse with his grandpa.
We made cookies for the whole class.

2.definition: A large number of things together.
sentences: There were so many stars in the sky.
She has read many books this year.
How many apples are in the basket?

2.definition: Away from or no longer on something.
sentences: Please turn off the lights when you leave.
She wiped the mud off her boots.
The lid fell off the jar and rolled away.

2.definition: Shows a choice between two different things.
sentences: Do you want pizza or pasta for dinner?
We can walk or take the bus.
Would you like juice or water?

2.definition: To grab something and bring it toward you.
sentences: Pull the door handle to open it.
She had to pull the heavy wagon uphill.
He helped pull the rope in the contest.

2.definition: To look at words and understand them.
sentences: I love to read books before bedtime.
She can read three chapters in one night.
He read the whole story without any help.

2.definition: The correct answer or opposite of left.
sentences: You got every single answer right.
Turn right at the stop sign.
She knew the right thing to do.

2.definition: To make music using your voice.
sentences: We love to sing songs around the campfire.
She can sing really high notes.
He started to sing on the way to school.

2.definition: To rest with your bottom on something.
sentences: Come sit next to me on the bench.
Please sit quietly during the movie.
She chose to sit near the window.

2.definition: To close your eyes and rest your body.
sentences: I sleep with my stuffed elephant every night.
She could not sleep because of the thunder.
Babies need a lot of sleep each day.

2.definition: To share information with another person.
sentences: Tell me all about your day at school.
She will tell the story from the beginning.
He forgot to tell his mom about the field trip.

2.definition: Belonging to a group of people or animals.
sentences: The dogs played happily in their big yard.
The kids finished all of their homework.
They cleaned up their side of the room.

2.definition: Points to more than one thing close to you.
sentences: These cookies just came out of the oven.
Are these your mittens on the floor?
She wants to keep all of these books.

2.definition: Points to more than one thing far from you.
sentences: Those mountains look incredibly tall today.
Do you know who owns those bikes?
She wants to try one of those someday.

2.definition: On top of or used to start a story.
sentences: Once upon a time lived a kind girl.
She placed the crown upon his head.
The cat climbed upon the highest shelf.

2.definition: Refers to yourself and others together.
sentences: Mom took all of us to the beach.
The teacher read to us after lunch.
Will you please wait for us?

2.definition: To do something with a tool or object.
sentences: I use a pencil to draw my pictures.
She showed him how to use the scissors.
We can use teamwork to solve hard problems.

2.definition: A much larger amount than usual.
sentences: That elephant is very very enormous.
She worked very hard on her project.
The soup is very hot so be careful.

2.definition: To clean something using soap and water.
sentences: Always wash your hands before eating dinner.
She helped wash the family car on Saturday.
He forgot to wash the mud off his boots.

2.definition: Asks you to choose between things.
sentences: Which flavor of ice cream do you want?
She could not decide which book to pick.
Which path should we take to get there?

2.definition: Asks for the reason something happened.
sentences: Why do birds fly south in the winter?
She wondered why the sky turns pink at sunset.
He asked why the plant stopped growing.

2.definition: To hope for something you really want.
sentences: I wish I could have a pet dragon.
She closed her eyes to make a wish.
He wished the rainy day would end soon.

2.definition: To do something that takes real effort.
sentences: We work together to keep our classroom clean.
She had to work hard to learn that skill.
Good things take a lot of work to build.

2.definition: Something that might possibly happen.
sentences: I would love to visit the ocean someday.
She said she would help after lunch.
He thought it would be a great surprise.

2.definition: To form letters and words on paper.
sentences: I write in my journal every single night.
She wants to write a book about her dog.
He learned to write his full name today.

2.definition: Something that belongs to you.
sentences: Is this your favorite song to sing?
Please put your backpack on the hook.
Your idea was the best one we heard.

3.definition: Having to do with a certain topic or subject.
sentences: I just finished a great book about dinosaurs.
She told me all about her trip to the mountains.
We learned about the water cycle in science class.

3.definition: More good than something else you are comparing.
sentences: I feel so much better after drinking water.
She got a better score on her second try.
Practice makes you better at almost everything.

3.definition: To carry something with you to another place.
sentences: Please remember to bring your lunch to school.
She will bring her favorite book to share.
He asked if he could bring a friend along.

3.definition: To hold something and move from place to place.
sentences: Can you carry this heavy box for me?
She had to carry all her supplies up the hill.
He learned to carry responsibility at a young age.

3.definition: Free from dirt and mess neat and fresh.
sentences: She always keeps a very clean bedroom.
We worked together to clean the whole classroom.
He made sure to clean up before his guests arrived.

3.definition: To use something sharp to divide an object.
sentences: Dad will cut the birthday cake for everyone.
She used scissors to cut the paper carefully.
He helped cut the vegetables for the soup.

3.definition: Completely finished with nothing more left to do.
sentences: I am finally done with all my homework.
She was done before the timer even went off.
When you are done reading please put the book away.

3.definition: To create a picture using a pencil or crayon.
sentences: I love to draw detailed animals at school.
She asked her dad to draw a map of the trail.
He can draw nearly anything you ask him to.

3.definition: To swallow a liquid such as water or juice.
sentences: I always drink water after I exercise.
She remembered to drink enough water on the hot day.
He poured himself a cold drink after the long walk.

3.definition: The number 8 which is one more than seven.
sentences: Did you know a spider has eight legs?
She has been reading for eight whole chapters.
There are eight planets in our solar system.

3.definition: To drop down or the season that comes after summer.
sentences: Colorful leaves fall gently from trees in autumn.
She watched the snowflakes fall outside her window.
He did not want to fall behind in his reading.

3.definition: A long distance away from where you are.
sentences: That star looks tiny because it is so far away.
She had traveled far from her hometown.
He could hear the music from far down the street.

3.definition: Holding as much as possible with no room left.
sentences: My backpack is completely full of library books.
She felt full after eating the big bowl of soup.
The auditorium was full for the school concert.

3.definition: Received or obtained something before now.
sentences: I got a perfect score on my spelling test.
She got the last book before the library closed.
He finally got the answer after thinking hard.

3.definition: To get bigger or taller naturally over time.
sentences: Plants always grow toward the warm sunlight.
She watched the tiny seed grow into a tall flower.
Reading every day helps your mind grow stronger.

3.definition: To keep something carefully in your hands or arms.
sentences: Always hold my hand when we cross the street.
She asked him to hold her place in line.
He tried to hold onto the rope during the game.

3.definition: Having a very high temperature opposite of cold.
sentences: The soup is still too hot to eat right now.
She stayed in the shade on the very hot afternoon.
The sand at the beach felt hot under our feet.

3.definition: To feel pain or to cause pain in someone.
sentences: My leg hurt after I fell off my bicycle.
She did not want to hurt her friend's feelings.
He made sure not to hurt the little caterpillar.

3.definition: Used when talking about something that might happen.
sentences: If it rains tomorrow we will play inside.
She asked if she could stay for dinner.
He wondered if the package would arrive on time.

3.definition: To hold onto something and not give it away.
sentences: You can keep the special book I gave you.
She tried to keep her room neat all week.
He made a promise and worked hard to keep it.

3.definition: Gentle caring and always nice to other people.
sentences: She is always so kind to new students.
Being kind to others can make their whole day better.
He chose to say something kind instead of something mean.

3.definition: The happy sound you make when something is funny.
sentences: That silly joke made absolutely everyone laugh.
She could not help but laugh at the funny story.
He started to laugh when the puppy sneezed.

3.definition: Brightness that helps you see or not very heavy.
sentences: The flashlight gave us enough light in the dark.
She chose the light backpack for the long hike.
He turned on the light to read before bed.

3.definition: A large distance from one end all the way to the other.
sentences: The giraffe has an incredibly long neck.
She had been waiting for a long time to see that show.
It was a long but very exciting journey home.

3.definition: A very large amount of something.
sentences: I had so much fun at the fair yesterday.
She worked so much harder than she ever had before.
How much time do we have left to finish?

3.definition: Doing something completely on your own without help.
sentences: I tied my own shoes by myself today.
She built the whole model by herself.
He taught himself to play three songs on the guitar.

3.definition: Not ever not even a single time.
sentences: I have never seen an actual live volcano.
She said she would never give up on her dream.
He had never tasted anything quite so delicious before.

3.definition: Just one single thing and absolutely nothing more.
sentences: I only have one piece of candy remaining.
She was the only one who knew the answer.
He had only a few minutes left to finish the test.

3.definition: Something that belongs to just you alone.
sentences: I have my very own special reading chair.
She was proud to have her own library card.
He worked hard to earn his own money.

3.definition: To choose one thing carefully from a whole group.
sentences: You can pick any book you want from the shelf.
She took her time to pick the perfect birthday card.
He could not pick just one favorite flavor.

3.definition: The number 7 which is one more than six.
sentences: There are exactly seven days in every week.
She read seven whole chapters before dinner.
He scored seven points in the basketball game.

3.definition: Something that will definitely and certainly happen.
sentences: We shall return home before it gets dark.
She told herself she shall never stop trying.
Shall we start reading the next chapter now?

3.definition: To let someone see or learn something new.
sentences: Please show me your amazing drawing when done.
She offered to show him how to solve the problem.
He was excited to show his project to the class.

3.definition: The number 6 which is one more than five.
sentences: A ladybug has exactly six tiny spotted legs.
She has six books checked out from the library.
They practiced the song six times before the show.

3.definition: Little in size not big at all.
sentences: The kitten was so small it fit in my hand.
She started with small steps and worked up to big ones.
Even small acts of kindness can make a big difference.

3.definition: To begin something for the very first time.
sentences: We start school promptly at eight in the morning.
She was nervous but decided to start anyway.
He read the first page to start the new chapter.

3.definition: The number 10 which is one more than nine.
sentences: I counted ten glowing fireflies in the yard.
She has ten favorite books she reads every year.
He ran ten laps around the track without stopping.

3.definition: The day that is currently happening right now.
sentences: Today is a wonderful and perfect day to read.
She decided that today was the day to try something new.
He smiled because today was finally his birthday.

3.definition: With each other doing something at the same time.
sentences: We built the whole puzzle together as a family.
She and her friend studied together every afternoon.
They decided to face the challenge together and won.

3.definition: To make a real effort to do something.
sentences: Always try your absolute best at everything you do.
She decided to try a new strategy on the hard problem.
He knew it was worth it to try one more time.

3.definition: A comfortable temperature not too hot and not too cold.
sentences: The soft blanket kept me perfectly warm all night.
She loved the warm feeling of sunshine on her face.
He poured a warm cup of cocoa on the cold morning.
